name: Forge-Father
title: the Anvil Beneath the Mountain
edicts: Honor every promise you make, no matter how small
Pass craft and lore to apprentices who have earned it
anathema: Break an oath sworn in your name
Destroy a masterwork that could be mended
desc: Patron of smiths, miners, and oathkeepers. Worshipped wherever hammer meets anvil and where families remember the names of those who built their walls. Forge-Father's clergy bind wounds in temple-forges and refuse no honest commission.

name: Veiled Dancer
title: the Step Between Worlds
edicts: Honor the threshold — never refuse a guest at your door, nor cross one uninvited
Find the truth no one is meant to see
anathema: Betray a secret kept under oath
Drag a hidden truth into the noonday sun without cause
desc: The Veiled Dancer walks the seams between waking and dreaming, public face and private heart. Mystics, scouts, diplomats, and thieves invoke the Dancer when they must move unseen — but the Dancer's true demand is honesty with oneself.

name: Eternal Flame
title: the Lamp That Does Not Gutter
edicts: Heal the wounded without weighing their merit
Speak truth even when it costs you
anathema: Refuse aid to the wounded out of malice or convenience
Knowingly spread a lie that harms the innocent
desc: The Eternal Flame is the lamp every traveler hopes to see at the end of the road. Healers, watchwomen, judges, and field medics carry its rites. Its clergy run free infirmaries in the worst quarters of any city that will tolerate them.

name: Pale Hunter
title: the Stalker in Winter
edicts: Take only what you can use — never more
Show respect for the worthy prey, in life and in death
Let the slow death of winter teach the proud
anathema: Hunt a creature that has surrendered or that you cannot kill cleanly
desc: The Pale Hunter does not bargain. Rangers, undertakers, executioners, and grieving widows leave offerings at frostbitten cairns. The Hunter teaches that every end is owed — only the manner of arriving is yours to choose.

name: Tidemother
title: She Who Returns
edicts: Honor the rhythm — what is given will be drawn back
anathema: Refuse a shipwrecked stranger food and dry ground
Pollute or poison a body of fresh water
desc: Sailors, midwives, river-folk, and farmers downstream of any flood-prone valley pray to the Tidemother. Her clergy run the lighthouses, the freshwater wells, and the orphanages — and they remember every face the tide once brought them.

name: Shadowed Throne
title: the Crown Beneath the Mountain
edicts: Honor the chain of command — above you and below you
Repay loyalty with loyalty, and treachery in kind
Make the law serve those it was written to serve
anathema: Break a binding contract you signed in good faith
Squander an inherited authority through neglect
desc: The Shadowed Throne does not require love. Magistrates, tax-collectors, infernal lawyers, and dynastic schemers find its rites where the law has teeth. Its temples are built into courthouses and counting-houses; its priests are the ones who write the contracts no one reads but everyone signs.
